danger_signals.visual_form: Bizarre creature designs: ghostly infants (JP), facehugger-like entities, creepy-crawly slug monsters, baby-like ghosts transformed into writhing biomass (US)
danger_signals.meaning: Hostile entities that require combat engagement; damage threat

danger_signals.visual_form: Increasingly surreal and grotesque environmental design; Stage 3 'Realm of Hungry Ghosts' features writhing biomass and removed faces on floating platforms
danger_signals.meaning: Escalating danger and supernatural/demonic threat intensity

danger_signals.visual_form: Larger, more detailed enemy sprites; unique visual identity distinct from normal enemy patterns
danger_signals.meaning: Major combat challenges requiring preparation and strategy

danger_signals.visual_form: Perilous realms with platforms and obstacles; Stage design complexity increases

safety_signals.visual_form: Stationary NPC character; shop interface with purchase menu (arrows replacing JP text 次/前)
safety_signals.meaning: Safe trading hub; currency (gold) exchange point for supplies and power-ups

safety_signals.visual_form: Graphic-based items dropped by defeated enemies (US version); text-based kanji powerups (JP); visual distinction from environment
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary stat/ability enhancement; temporary safety buffers (health, invulnerability, enhanced weapons)

safety_signals.visual_form: Dog character model; summoned via mechanic; distinct sprite design
safety_signals.meaning: Ally damage output; mechanical support system during combat

safety_signals.visual_form: Sword weapon sprite; three crystal choices (Flight, Life, Spirit visual variants)
safety_signals.meaning: Base offensive capability; player chooses permanent progression direction based on crystal visual/effect selection

faction_indicators.faction: Kingdom of the Crystal Palace (legitimate rulers)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Crystal Palace architecture; Crown symbol implied in 'King Zaras' usurpation narrative
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Crystal Palace endgame location; multiple realms as conquered territories; stage progression represents reclaimed domains

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: All 'bizarre enemies' serve Zaras; grotesque aesthetic dominates stages as visual corruption of kingdom
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Demonic/supernatural stage themes ('Realm of Hungry Ghosts', 'Demon-Heaven'); surreal environmental design; writhing biomass (US Stage 3 redesign); overall aesthetic decay
Shop locations; NPC safe zones; Kim as news reporter reinforces intelligence gathering for restoration cause
Kidnapped/imprisoned location in palace; liberation objective; reward mechanic signals political restoration narrative

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Grotesque, demonic stage design; Stage 3 'Realm of Hungry Ghosts' (JP) with baby ghost imagery (Edvard Munch-inspired disturbing aesthetic); writhing biomass; enemy-filled environments; corrupted architecture
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies actively patrol and attack; no safe zones except shop locations; NPCs offer hints/commerce rather than military aid; Zap's primary role as combat companion reflects resistance cell structure

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Each stage defeated represents reclaimed realm; Crystal Princess rescue as final stage marker; five perilous realms structure as conquered territories narrative
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy defeat removes threat from stage zones; power-up acquisition increases Farron's capability (visual/mechanical feedback); Kim's shop restocks suggesting expanding supply lines; narrative progression toward Crystal Palace final confrontation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Stage 3 complete overhaul: baby ghost heads → writhing biomass; creepy-crawly slug monster redesign; facehugger-like entity variant (removed baby-like aesthetic); floating platforms with removed faces; title screen adds Asmik mascot and mountains; reduced kanji usage in UI
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Reduced difficulty; added cheat codes (full health restoration, invulnerability, extra lives); enemy hit-point reduction; speech bubble changed from 'Over here' (Japanese guidance) to 'Hi!' (generic greeting)

environmental_storytelling.example: Stage 3 'Realm of Hungry Ghosts' (餓鬼界, JP) — naming establishes metaphysical/demonic setting; visual design (writhing biomass, distorted faces) communicates suffering and supernatural corruption as world-state under Zaras

Crystal Palace as endgame location; multiple 'perilous realms' structure implies Zaras controls fragmented territories; palace reclamation objective narrativizes player progression through visual destination signposting

Grotesque, bizarre creatures serve as visual manifestation of Zaras's corrupting rule; no naturalistic wildlife — all enemies are supernatural/demonic, communicating unnatural oppression of kingdom ecosystem

Kim doubles as shopkeeper AND 'hint-dropping news reporter' — positioning communicates underground information network; Zap as dog companion (faithful servant) reinforces loyalty theme; Crystal Princess imprisonment as literal political hostage situation

Three magical crystals (Flight/Life/Spirit) represent kingdom's resources/magic system; Moon Mirror as rescue reward establishes princess as both damsel and source of additional power, reinforcing restoration narrative

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Sword attacks as primary method; scrolls provide long-range abilities; movement and jump mechanics form base traversal
Increased jump height; vertical traversal advantage
Unlimited fireball attack; continuous ranged offense capability
Deals damage to enemies; temporal summon mechanic suggests resource management (cooldown/cost implied)
Attack on sight; defeated by repeated sword strikes (US version reduces hit requirement); drop power-ups upon defeat (visual loot mechanic)
Provides commerce interface; speech bubble changes from JP 'Over here' to US 'Hi!' (contextual greeting shift); doubles as information source ('hint-dropping news reporter')
Kidnapped quest objective; bestows Moon Mirror upon rescue (reward NPC state change)

notes: Conquest of the Crystal Palace demonstrates significant regional manifestation differences between JP and US versions. JP version uses kanji extensively (stage names, shop menus, powerup text) and employs disturbing surreal imagery (Edvard Munch-inspired 'Realm of Hungry Ghosts', baby ghost enemies) to communicate supernatural danger and cultural otherness. US version replaces kanji with English and redesigns grotesque enemies/stages into generic sci-fi/biological variants (facehugger-like entity, biomass). This represents editorial localization of world-state communication: JP emphasizes demonic/supernatural corruption through Japanese aesthetic markers; US emphasizes alien/biological threat through sci-fi visual language. Both convey Zaras's illegitimate rule through environmental grotesqueness, but via different cultural visual vocabularies. The three-crystal choice system at game start functions as permanent faction/playstyle commitment, visually signifying player's restoration strategy selection. Zap's loyal companion mechanic and Kim's dual role (commerce + intelligence) suggest underground resistance infrastructure. Story positions Farron as rightful prince with magical artifacts (crystals) against usurper Zaras with corrupted realm control — standard restoration narrative with faction identity clearly marked through architectural/environmental design rather than explicit banner/color coding.
